decision.text:
Stop. Output ONLY this template, filled in, no other text:

LAB: <lab_slug>
CLAW: <claw_id>
OUTCOME: <abandon|keep_evidence|continue|merge|publish|graduate_to_spine>
WHY: <one sentence>

Use the actual lab slugs and claw IDs from the federation. Pick the single highest-priority awaiting claw.
decision.reason: Three prior chats failed to yield concrete lab+claw+outcome; demand a rigid template to unblock step 2.

workflow_judgment.reasoning: The persona executed 4 of 6 canonical steps: opened chat with chief (turns 0-4), expanded a lab (turn 12, cgl-publish), promoted a claw (turn 14), and attempted decision verification via view_result (turn 15). However, the chief never produced a clear prioritized recommendation despite 5 chat attempts, so step 2 (identify highest-priority lab from chief) was not satisfied — the persona abandoned chat and picked cgl-publish opportunistically. Step 4 (read lab agent's auto-switched chat view on the awaiting claw) was attempted but the transcript shows view_result/keypress fumbling without confirmation the lab agent view was actually read. Critically, the artifact pre-check shows no decision.json was written, indicating the promote action silently failed — a critical finding per the workflow notes.
